title: new uidmap BATS test: fix
body:
Various problems, one of which was causing the test to fail
completely (otherwise I wouldn't have caught the others):

- option is --uidmap, not --uidmapping
- run_podman cannot be piped (| grep /sys/kernel). That's
  an unfortunate limitation of BATS. Any invocation of 'run'
  saves results to $output, which then has to be tested
  in a separate step.
  - do so.
- remove "$expected_rc", that looks like a copy/paste bug
  from a few lines above.

We really need to get these tests running in CI.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Test is failing in rootless. @giuseppe please advise:
```
$ ./bin/podman run --rm --uidmap 0:100:10000 --net=host alpine mount|grep /ker                                                      uidmap_test_fix (1)
securityfs on /sys/kernel/security type securityfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
configfs on /sys/kernel/config type configfs (rw,relatime)
debugfs on /sys/kernel/debug type debugfs (rw,seclabel,relatime)
tmpfs on /sys/kernel type tmpfs (ro,seclabel,relatime,uid=1345283,gid=1345283)
```
That test will fail as rootless, since it can only umount them in root mode.
Thanks. New force-push, skips test if rootless. Commit message updated accordingly.
